rationales.hook_strength: 'Organ of longevity' reframe is specific and contrarian, but 'nobody frames it this way' undersells itself — the framing is actually common in longevity circles.
rationales.clarity: Each slide owns exactly one idea with a clear mechanism label and a single bolded takeaway — nothing to re-read.
rationales.audience_fit: Steady blood sugar and two sessions/week lands well for busy 20–35s, but 'falls and frailty' skews toward a 50+ fear set rather than the tired-by-3pm crowd.
rationales.novelty: 'Muscle is your glucose sink' is a fresher angle, but protein-spreading and two-sessions-a-week advice is well-worn territory on health TikTok.
rationales.brand_fit: Voice is plain and direct, claims are appropriately hedged ('can reverse much of that'), CTA ties the content to the score without hype.
rationales.predicted_performance: High save potential on the mechanism slides; sharing is moderate because the hook, while solid, won't provoke the 'send this to someone' impulse.
